title: Yandex Translate API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Yandex Translate API key detected. This key provides authenticated access to Yandex Translation services including text translation, language detection, supported languages lookup, and translation quality assessment. Unauthorized access can lead to API quota abuse, service billing fraud, potential data mining of sensitive translation content, and exposure of confidential multilingual communications. Immediately revoke this key from your Yandex Developer console and rotate credentials using secure environment variables.

Yandex Translate API token detected. This token grants access to Yandex Translation services with potentially extended permissions including batch translation, premium language pairs, and advanced translation features. Exposing Yandex Translate tokens enables unauthorized translation service usage, API quota theft, and potential interception of sensitive multilingual content. Revoke immediately from your Yandex Developer account and implement secure credential storage.

Yandex Translate client ID or application identifier detected. These identifiers are used for client authentication and application registration with Yandex Translation services. While less sensitive than API keys, exposing client IDs can reveal application configuration details, supported language pairs, and enable targeted attacks against your Yandex Translate integrations. Store securely using environment variables or configuration management.
